data-uuid="3aae3e49-bd60-423b-848a-81dc70fbd3eb">O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Internal Structure of Workplace diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display Properties and/or Ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button_MD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="8eeb3a45-a5b8-4417-8928-9b78a5dd1bb5"><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span>After selecting the required command from the</span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="2b386156-9ec7-4b5f-83e9-597aa03028af">Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="0fb35603-e2f7-4197-81d3-6209cecd43ee">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Properties and Ports of the PC Part shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_command_group_MD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="d11067c1-27f3-4bbf-8941-8878cb77bd2a"><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the Display command menu, the appropriate properties, Ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide Ports</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="b9565b7d-5069-44a9-8e7a-6a576b212979">Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and, o<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="aa73e90d-b087-40bb-b182-caeb52c033df"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the command, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Hide All Ports command under the Display command group from the shortcut menu of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports_shortcut_menu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="uml_diagrams.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, only first-level ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> Follow the steps below to learn how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="be4c447a-f36d-4bb2-ba4c-27f9efc36efe">Select<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> the required</span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li data-uuid="6a27345a-15b6-4b46-89ad-89b9f757b4b6"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span>All first-level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. " ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the PC Part shape. "><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports_SM_MD.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> in the Composite Structure diagram</span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The most efficient way to create a new property and display it with all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> is by dragging the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Class" /></ac:link> or Property elements. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This method does not allow you to display deeply nested properties, only first-level properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. The following procedures will show you:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="5c766155-b70b-4896-9269-39eef688bdc4"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Property shape and how to display all its Ports when dragging the Class element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="d379c283-ad8b-4981-a54b-304ffe748c8e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">Ports</span> by dragging the Property element.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create a new Property by dragging the Class element <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">from the <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Browser" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Model Browser</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane</span>. The newly created Property will be typed by the dragged Class and displayed on the diagram pane. If this Class has Ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></p><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To create and display a Property by dragging a Class element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="5dcfafd5-bec1-4ff7-86f2-7c8ffd02ec04"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="49e4afa6-cbdc-424a-9e3e-a78facc3314a"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). <span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol </strong>command.</span><br />The new Property is created in the model and displayed on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p>To create and display a Property and display all its Ports by dragging a Class element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="1a41cf41-b005-4a72-8dd0-bda9f3bd5ffb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Class element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li data-uuid="857a9454-2e10-4b1b-baa4-b5186de00496"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new Property is created in the model and displayed with all its Ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Property is typed by the dragged Class automatically.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d407cdbc-1a05-485b-9d7d-bd7aec91f448"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command and </span>dragging (long drag) the Class element to <em>Internal Structure of Workplace</em> diagram pane, it </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">creates a new Property typed by a </span><em>Duplex Power Outlet</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Class and displays all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_class_on-diagram_pane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can display a Property shape by dragging a Property element from the Model Browser to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> pane. If this property has <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined, you can display them by using the long drag and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following procedures describe how to display Property shape and all its Ports by dragging the Property element.</span></p><p>To display a Property by dragging a Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="e47f4805-d0dd-4c88-b15d-5c9683f15fd4">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="94325791-88f1-4260-a189-5ce7def8b598">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span>Drop it, and from the opened menu, select the<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong> Create Property Symbol </strong></span>command.</span><br />The Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To display a Property with all of its Ports by dragging a Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="31380498-453c-42f9-8c8e-8704989827bc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). </span></li><li data-uuid="7ad1f896-bdbe-44be-a75f-4191476421b5"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Property shape is displayed with all its Ports on that shape.<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="00be8f45-9eb5-4f84-b3c5-e89d3731db2e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Property element to</span><em> Internal Structure of Workplace</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> diagram, it displays Property shape and all Ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After completing the steps outlined above, the Property shape and/or all its Ports are displayed on the Composite Structure diagram.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Port</h3><p>If you display properties of a Port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Port shape and part compartments will be enabled for that Port.</p><p>To enable Part compartments on the Port shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="60c348f9-d515-4367-b010-2cc24754ab57">Select a Port shape whose type has properties.</li><li data-uuid="705e6bd7-03cb-4625-b1f4-d55df0688917">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="2181cf10-bf64-4a4c-bc37-1ec9fe67d7ec">Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the Port shape. Part compartments are enabled for the Port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> Port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the Port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected Port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> Port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target" data-uuid="f63fe262-9824-46df-b8ba-2824fcd4a8be"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying elements" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1ed80d57-6536-4bb5-8abc-ca64034fc28b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li data-uuid="25a07c05-27c6-4f97-bd9c-e10afef95d6b"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Nesting parts that are association ends" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="e7cb164a-6a45-4de1-9a0b-3ec846c043fd"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d775be15-17b1-4ee7-b6da-a6a72b13249d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Class diagram" /></ac:link>  </li><li data-uuid="2d8f1f0d-46cf-4168-828e-ee91568bd85c"><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Composite Structure diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li data-uuid="3314ba28-4adc-4f49-b698-7ceea6760e8e"><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Component diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li data-uuid="925accf1-e615-4911-b702-5777ac1d1a08"><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Package diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li data-uuid="d0841cfd-7827-466c-bf2f-dbf40663a12c"><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Deployment diagram" /></ac:link> </span></li><li data-uuid="d9b96b58-1c0a-4c38-a43f-b3c86b37147a"><span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagram ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="448a4212-0a9f-4b15-8b0b-884684c3e8fe">The <strong>Composite structure diagram </strong>sample model. To open this sample go to <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730322 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02994: (2026x) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects

- page_id: `272730322`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730322/2026x+Displaying+parts+and+ports+according+to+aspects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:36:57.852+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581472387

581472391

581472390

Before using this feature, make sure:

- The diagram with aspect is created. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram aspects' space='']>]]>
- The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) How aspects match with parts and ports' space='']>]]>

When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical *Data Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the **All By Aspect** button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected *control Panel* part.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

581472386

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model, which you can find:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bcd8e89b-1f9f-408a-b6f6-affd9427300f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472387</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3df8ea34-f6f7-4cf8-a5ad-f360b21222b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472391</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cee8920-b70e-421a-aeb8-05dcb0da208a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before using this feature, make sure:</p><ul><li>The diagram with aspect is created. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Diagram aspects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The main workflow of using aspects >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>The part and/or port stereotype names matches the aspect name. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) How aspects match with parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Matching rules >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>When displaying Parts/Ports in the aspect diagram, e.g., Electrical <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Data Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram, you can display all the parts/ports of an appropriate aspect, e.g., Electrical, by using the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">All By Aspect</strong> button. After selecting this button, all parts or ports that match the diagram's aspect can be displayed. The figure below demonstrates the usage of this button in the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you are trying to display all electrical aspect ports of the selected <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">control Panel</em> part.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="all_by_aspect.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports according to aspects" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The All By Aspect button usage in the Display Parts/Ports dialog. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a59f1e42-587f-49d8-9347-83280f66505c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581472386</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model, which you can find:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">samples\SysML v1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731113 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02995: (2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1

- page_id: `272731113`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731113/2026x+Displaying+parts+and+ports+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:38:20.150+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x) Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports', 'enable-part-compartments', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display ports, properties, and deep nested ports and properties that have already been created in all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML diagram descriptions' space=''].This functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allow you to display properties that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED'] have been applied to.If you have applied your own stereotypes for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about creating custom stereotypes and applying them, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML Profiling and DSL Guide' space='UPDG'].

##### Ways to display parts and ports

###### Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog

You can use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog****opens automatically when creating a new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] element that owns ports and properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructure diagrams that have already been created.The**Display Parts/Ports** dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] , ports , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select the required parts , ports , and other properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose the deeply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Nesting parts that are association ends' space=''] parts , ports , and other properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for parts .
- Specify layout options for ports .

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following areas:

- *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of a context [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']Block.
- *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select parts/ports in the Element tree. You can select all, clear all, or choose properties only by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- *Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and portswhen they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure will guide you in working with the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you want to display required ports and properties on the diagram pane.

To display ports and properties using the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog

1. Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML diagram descriptions' space='']structure diagram , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes. O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parts/Ports**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parts/Ports**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the Display Parts/Ports dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display, using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf certain check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:- Manually select individual check boxes one by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All properties (in this example all ports*AirFlowPort, ElectricityPort, AutomotiveBusesPort*) of the *Air Distribution* Part Property are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Ports** group - displays all port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All Electrical ports (in this example only one *ElectricityPort* port) of the *Data Transfer* Part Property are selected in the element tree by using the **Select Ports** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Parts layout: select the Layout Parts check box if you want the Part Property shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: select the Use Layout Template check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part Property, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Showing connectors: select the Show Connectors check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.
  - Ports layout on Part Property shape: - Top/Bottom - displays ports on the Part Property shape from top to bottom. [ATTACHMENT filename='port_layout_top_bottom.png'] - Left/Right - displays ports on the Part Property shape from left to right. [ATTACHMENT filename='port_layout_left_right.png']
5. Click **OK**.

After those steps, all parts, ports, other properties that were selected in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display ports, properties, and hide all ports by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the **Display**button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commands are interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected Part Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures will show you how to:

- How to display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button.
- 
- 

**button**To display ports, properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_element.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select required properties or ports to display: 
 - All ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display ports, properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display_command.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first-level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the **Display** command menu, the appropriate properties' ports are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide ports

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes. O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Ports**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select **Display** >**Hide All Ports**. 
After selecting the command, the portsof the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane, but not removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Ports** command doesn't appear, the selection or diagramdoesn't contain any portsthat can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the properties are already displayed on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML diagram descriptions' space=''], you can display their direct ports using the **Display All Ports** button from the smart manipulator toolbar. It only allows you to display first level ports, and does not allow you to display deeply nested ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The steps below show how to display ports of a selected property shape.

To display Ports on shapes

1. Select the required element shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane. WarningIf the **Display All Ports** button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoesn't own any Port. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the drag-and-drop operation in the SysML Internal Block diagram

The most efficient way to create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] and display it with all ports in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']SysML Internal Block diagram is by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or Property elements. This method only allows you to display first-level properties, and does not allow you to display deeply nested properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The following procedures will show you:

- 
- How to display a Part Property shape and all its ports by dragging the Part Property element.

drag_classYou can create a new Part Property by dragging the Blockelement from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space='']to the SysML Internal Block diagram pane. The newly created Part Property will be typed by the dragged Block and displayed on the diagram pane. If this Block has ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3).

****

To create and display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] by dragging a Block element

- Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane,and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol** command. 
The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']. The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.

To create and display a Part Property and display all its ports by dragging a Block element

- Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed with all its ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following: 
 - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the **Select in Containment Tree** command. 
 - Select the shape and press Alt+B.

#### TIP: Example 3

Example 3

After selecting the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command, anddragging (long drag) the Block element to *Control and Air Distribution Group* Internal Block diagram pane, itcreates a new Part Property typed by a *Air Distribution* Block and displays all ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

drag_propertyYou can display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] shape by dragging a Part Property element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] pane. If this Part Property has ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).

The following procedures describe how to display Part Property shape and all its ports by dragging the Part Property element.

To display a Part Property shape by dragging a Part Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space=''] .
- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) . Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Part Property Symbol** command. The Part Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane .

To display a Part Property with all of its ports by dragging a Part Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) .
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The Part Property shape is displayed with all its ports on that shape.

#### TIP: Example 4

Example 4

After selecting the**Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports**command, and dragging (long drag) the Part Property element to*Control and Air Distribution Group*Internal Block diagram, it displays Part Property shape and all ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']shape and/or all its ports are displayed on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

##### Displaying properties of a port

If you display the properties of a port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the port shape and Part Property compartments will be enabled for that port.

To enable Part Property compartments on the port shape

1. Select a port shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the port shape. The Part Property compartments are enabled for the port.

The example below shows the *p1* port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the port shape are the *Pressure Status* Flow Property and the *suspensionSensor* Part Property, which are the properties of the selected port type *iSuspension* Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the *p1* port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Sample model**

The models used in the figures of this page are:

- The Vehicle Climate Control Unit sample model. To open this sample d ownload [ATTACHMENT filename='Vehicle Climate Control Unit.mdzip'] . **
- The **Vehicle parts under ports** sample model. To open this sample download [ATTACHMENT filename='Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip'].

ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="029c098c-d73a-4b73-aaed-2bf830f68f44"><p>Choose the properties you want to display, using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If certain <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">check boxes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> are inactive in the element list, </span>those elements are already <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">displayed</span> in the diagram.<br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the element tree located on the left side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- Manually select individual check boxes one by one.  <br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58147caf-ac97-47ac-bfdd-939da1408e1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All properties (in this example all ports<em> AirFlowPort, ElectricityPort, AutomotiveBusesPort</em>) of the <em>Air Distribution</em> Part Property are selected recursively <span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span> from the element tree</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested properties in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Use the context-sensitive panel located on the right side of the dialog:</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">(including your own <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span> that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> group - displays all port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.<br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fa4ff9-c35d-49d7-855f-d85a81261e84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All Electrical ports (in this example only one <em>ElectricityPort</em> port) of the <em>Data Transfer</em> Part Property are selected <span>in the element tree by using the <span><strong>Select Ports</strong> group</span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_context_sensitive_panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0c896059-7faf-4250-883f-0103ccc569cb"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part Property shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">select the </span><strong>Use Layout Template</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part Property, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</span></li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li><li>Ports layout on Part Property shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from top to bottom.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_top_bottom.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from left to right.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_left_right.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li data-uuid="0d3d3db7-c16d-46be-ab2c-468be107608c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After those steps, all parts, ports, other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, <span class="confluence-link">properties</span>, and hide all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="e6e1d3fc-c7d6-4b7c-a105-3c9f9e0b041e">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="6600c34c-0eed-4e2d-8943-0cb5c9bac986">The <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>the <strong>Display </strong>button</span></span> and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from the shortcut menu as</span> their functions are identical. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">All those commands are interactive and <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains only the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu shows commands that can be applied to the whole diagram.</span></span></p><p>These <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are comprised of </span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li data-uuid="05b9e036-9464-43c2-9c44-75f182a59a0f">Metatypes.</li><li data-uuid="3720ecd7-8c5b-4db5-9366-dedd6a3672f3">Stereotypes.</li><li data-uuid="5928f08c-5d39-45b5-85eb-a730b1dc0d9a">Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="d5c7da92-ea38-4251-844a-b1e9beb2cac1"><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="3e64e3e6-7600-43c8-8d83-6b6de46a6f7a"><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="436a6722-c96f-4fbe-af19-14f5785cc9f3"><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="3ff476de-a712-4490-97e3-ed593d2c7bfb">Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="de698ecb-3211-4946-92a7-444e6fe049b3">O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Data Transfer and Control Groups Internal Block diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display properties and/or ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="f899dab3-a96a-46b8-bc98-21bb5f0a13d7"><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties or ports to display:<br />        - All ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span>After selecting the required command from the</span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="7ee4d15f-ea13-44a4-8394-c255fc425310">Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li data-uuid="3b575662-48fd-4a89-9c63-ec36b27a7beb">From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_command.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li data-uuid="8022b43e-2bf0-4a33-ba0f-7ba39621bdc9"><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first-level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the <strong>Display</strong> command menu, the appropriate properties' ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide ports</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="42afebbf-54ab-4dc3-8119-14fe5a6464f7">Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes. O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="4d20f7a7-f582-4591-84b5-e2c8914a682c"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the command, the <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane, but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:title="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML diagram descriptions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">It only allows you to display first level ports, and does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> The steps below show how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="45be4179-a173-40f7-8517-93b2db25a32d">Select<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span> the required</span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li data-uuid="ba56b495-b99c-424b-8ddb-5d7b4e95b5a2"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span>All first level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span> <br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation in the SysML Internal Block diagram</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The most efficient way to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> and display it with all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">SysML Internal Block diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> is by dragging the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></span> or Property elements. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">This method only allows you to display first-level properties, and does not allow you to display deeply nested properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. The following procedures will show you:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="2f10c228-5188-4b01-a67a-cdabe1522219"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Part Property shape and how to display all its ports when dragging the Block element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li><li data-uuid="1937fb34-e4ec-496b-9487-0690c4c6ade7"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can create a new Part Property by dragging the <span class="confluence-link">Block </span>element <span class="confluence-link">from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Model Browser" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>to the SysML Internal Block diagram pane</span>. The newly created Part Property will be typed by the dragged <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></p><p><strong> </strong></p><p>To create and display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> by dragging a Block element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="92ce0694-1f28-4901-a230-babfc6faf2d4"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li data-uuid="8ad923f2-33cc-49d0-be83-f80e2a1a34d4"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, </span>and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag). <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol</strong> command.</span><br />The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.</span></span> </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p>To create and display a Part Property and display all its ports by dragging a Block element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="0860d016-011b-4de0-a300-263df149682f"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drag a single or a multiple Block element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li data-uuid="81620624-add5-4573-8f33-72bc51bd7c49"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The new Part Property is created in the model, and displayed with all its ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a3ba930-c9af-4a64-8b14-28f87fe613a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command, and</span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">dragging (long drag) the Block element to <em>Control and Air Distribution Group</em> Internal Block diagram pane, it</span> </span>creates a new Part Property typed by a <em>Air Distribution</em> Block and displays all ports on it.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> shape by dragging a Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> pane. If this Part Property has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).<br /></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The following procedures describe how to display Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To display a Part Property shape by dragging a Part Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="067f13c3-1334-4ad6-8f63-bebaaa7fe512">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li data-uuid="07d66c1a-a294-4bf7-8c11-80ba29524de2">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol</strong> command. </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Part Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane</span>.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To display a Part Property with all of its ports by dragging a Part Property element</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="8318c4af-e706-486f-b747-b2ce5b0fadfb">Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the Model Browser onto the diagram pane, and hold it down <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></li><li data-uuid="3f9d2ce5-6bf4-4d5b-bc7c-1cf329126c9c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Part Property shape is displayed with all its ports on that shape.<br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bbc855a-c19c-4afe-8c7c-56e981ef78a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 4</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Part Property element to</span><em> <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Control and Air Distribution Group</span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Internal Block diagram, it displays Part Property shape and all ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_part_property.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After completing the steps outlined above, the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Property ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">shape and/or all its ports are displayed on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></p><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying properties of a port</h3><p>If you display the properties of a port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the port shape and Part Property compartments will be enabled for that port.</p><p>To enable Part Property compartments on the port shape</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="80e27c1f-2712-4675-a3bf-5fc7c771bb2d">Select a port shape whose type has properties.</li><li data-uuid="5b172f13-c700-4c92-be0c-8275d490e264">Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="c9600801-5ac2-4287-8f41-5e78a630093a">Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the port shape. The Part Property compartments are enabled for the port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying parts and ports in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section
