<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733962 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02996: (2026x) Displaying paths

- page_id: `272733962`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733962/2026x+Displaying+paths
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:13.845+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Displaying elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality allows you to visualize relationships that are created between elements in the model, but are not displayed on the diagram pane. There are two options to display paths among the element shapes:

- 
- 

displayedTo display paths between selected element shapes

1. On a diagram pane, select two or more elements.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display In-Between Paths .
  - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display In-Between Paths .

- 
  - On the main menu, click the **Analyze** > **Display In-Between Paths**. The **Display In-Between Paths** command is available only when two or more shapes are selected.

The existing paths between selected element shapes are displayed on the diagram pane.

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

The paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane. 
Paths to self also will not be displayed.

not_displayedTo display all existing paths of the selected shape

1. On a diagram pane, select one or more elements.
2. Do one of the following: All the existing paths among the selected shape and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed. If the element has a path to self, this path will be displayed as well.
  1. 
    - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display All Paths .
    - On the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] and select Display All Paths .
  - 
    - On the main menu, click the Analyze > Display All Paths .

#### TIP: Example of the Display All Paths command

Example of the Display All Paths command

This example presents the workflow on representing the paths between shapes on the diagram pane when they are already created in the model.

The Class shapes without any paths between them are represented in the *Room infrastructure model* Class diagram. In this case, paths between those classes exist in the Containment tree under the Relations node (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you want to visualize how the *Workplace* Class is related with other shapes displayed on the diagram pane, you can do so easily by using the**Display All Paths** command. One way to reach this command is from the shortcut menu of the selected shape (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After selecting the**Display All Paths** command, all paths between the *Workplace* Class and other shapes on the diagram pane are displayed (see the figure below). The diagram shows that the *Room* consists of between one and an arbitrary number of *Workplaces*. The *Workplace* consists of *Electric Extension* and *Works Station*. Also, the *Workplace* can contain *Duplex Power Outlet*, *External HDD2*, *Digital Camera*, and *External_HDD*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display paths among element shapes automatically when displaying elements

1. On the main menu, select Options > Environment .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Customizing environment options' space=''] , find the Display Paths on Element Drop option and set it's value to true .
3. Click OK .
4. Select the elements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] and drag them on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Understanding the user interface' space='']. The shapes and paths between them are displayed. WarningThe paths won't be displayed if: 
 - The selected shape doesn't have any relationships with the shapes displayed on the diagram. 
 - All paths are already displayed on the diagram pane.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Working with paths and relationships' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='DEVG']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Composite structure diagram** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='composite structure diagram.mdzip'].
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\composite structure diagram.mdzip.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 02997: (2026x) Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on selected Operational Activity Edge

- page_id: `272732849`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732849/2026x+Displaying+possible+Operational+Exchanges+on+selected+Operational+Activity+Edge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:20.228+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF > (2026x) NATO Operational Viewpoint > (2026x) NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge

1. Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the Operational Exchange Manager button.
2. Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.

You can also click the **Operational Exchange** button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the **Operational Exchange** creation wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge</p><hr /><ol><li>Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> button.</li><li>Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> creation wizard.</p><p><br /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge

1. Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the Operational Exchange Manager button.
2. Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.

You can also click the **Operational Exchange** button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the **Operational Exchange** creation wizard.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735938 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 02999: (2026x) Displaying rake icon

- page_id: `272735938`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735938/2026x+Displaying+rake+icon
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:28.069+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Formatting symbols > (2026x) Displaying icon or image
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use [IMAGE alt='' src=''] for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.

The rake icon is shown on:

- Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.
- Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.
- Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.
- Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.
- Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol and select Symbol Properties or press Alt+Enter.
2. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use <ac:image ac:title="Rake" ac:alt="Rake"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>  for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element is decomposed and has an inner structure. Double-click the element with rake icon to open the internal diagram.</p><p>The rake icon is shown on:</p><ul><li>Call Behavior actions that can refer to another Activity diagram.</li><li>Interaction Uses that can refer to another Sequence diagram.</li><li>Packages that can refer to another Package diagrams.</li><li>Parts that can refer to another Internal Block diagram.</li><li>Use cases can be realized by other behavior diagrams such as Activity, State Machine and Sequence.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="Rake icon on symbol" ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying rake icon" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol and select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732334 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03000: (2026x) Displaying Rake icon on symbol

- page_id: `272732334`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732334/2026x+Displaying+Rake+icon+on+symbol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:27.387+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Working with model elements in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol.
2. Select Symbol Properties from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.
3. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.</p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol.</li><li>Select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to false.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol" ac:title="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying Rake icon on symbol" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733969 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03001: (2026x) Displaying related elements

- page_id: `272733969`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733969/2026x+Displaying+related+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:14.005+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming > (2026x) Displaying elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to extend your model or visualize additional elements on the diagram pane, you can represent symbols that are related to a selected symbol via relationships.This functionality allows for displaying shapes and paths among those shapes which have already been created in the model.

After selecting the **Display Related Elements** command, the **Display Related Elements** dialog opens. This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):

- Element box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.
- Relations area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.
- Expand Relations area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.
- Scope area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.
- Depth area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.
- Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.
- Layout related elements option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The following procedure will guide you through using the **Display Related Elements** command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. The functionality works for the entire diagram, single shapes, or numerous selected shapes.

To display related elements

1. Select a diagram frame or any number of shapes on the diagram pane.
2. Open the **Display Related Elements** dialog in one of the following ways:
  - Right-click to open the shortcut menu, and select Display > Display Related Elements .
  - On the diagram toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_elements.png'] , and select Display Related Elements .
  - On the main menu, click **Analyze** > **Display Related Elements**. ImportantThe **Display Related Elements** dialog cannot be opened if the selected element shape or diagram doesn't contain any related elements. Example 1A quicker way to display related elements of the selected shape is to use its shortcut menu, and select **Display** > **Display Related Elements**. The example below demonstrates the shortcut menu of the Class named *Shipment*. It is showing the need to display related elements of the *Shipment* Class in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened dialog, specify:
  - In the Relations area (2), select check boxes of the listed relationship types.
  - In the Expand Relations area (3), select the direction of the relationships: - Incoming - in which the element participates as client. - Outgoing - in which the element participates as supplier. - Both- in which the element participates as both client and supplier.
  - In the Scope area (4), choose the location in which the related elements should be found: - Whole Project - search in the whole model. - Package - search in the package under which the selected element is created.
  - In the Depth area (5), select the search range for the relationships: - Indefinite - search for all possible relationships. - Definite - search for a specified level of relationships.
4. Select the check box of the Always create new symbols option to create a symbol even when the related element already has a symbol displayed on the diagram pane.
5. Select the check box of the **Layout related elements**option to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout' space=''] new symbols by using the default diagram [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout' space='']. NoteThe position of the selected element shape can be changed after selecting the **Layout related elements** check box. Example 2For example, if you would like to display all related elements of the *Shipment* Class that are connected through the Generalization relationship, you can find them according to the following criteria:- The direction of the generalization relation is both incoming and outgoing. 
- The related elements to be searched for are in the whole project. 
- The search depth of the relationship is one level.If you want to layout related elements after the display, but don't want to create new symbols which are already displayed in the *Product Shipment object diagram*.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Click **OK**. 
According to the specified relationship type, scope, depth, direction, layout, and creation options, all related elements of the selected shape are represented as shapes and paths on the diagram pane. Example 3After opening the **Display Related Elements** dialog (Example 1), and defining the search criteria in it (Example 2), two Classes (*VendorShipment* and *CustomerShipment*) related with the Shipment Class via the Generalization relationship are displayed on the *Product Shipment object diagram* pane. The figure below is the result of following the steps described in Example 1 and Example 2.[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Displaying elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Layout' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements in the diagram' space='DEVG'] (Open API)

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Inventory Control System** sample model. To open this sample go to*<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\case studies\Inventory Control System.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

This dialog consists of the following sections (see the figure below):</p><ul><li data-uuid="ca7df487-8f30-4cea-9917-74db4fa3bac6"><em>Element</em> box (1) shows the name of the selected element shape of which you are going to display its related elements.</li><li data-uuid="672d4acd-fc2d-464c-a1ae-054ce7bc06b9"><em>Relations</em> area (2) lists all relationship types which connect the selected element shape with other elements.</li><li data-uuid="f82802c3-4aa4-46b6-8f8b-3b971d9850a6"><em>Expand Relations</em> area (3) allows you to select the direction of the relationships which you want to display on the diagram pane.</li><li data-uuid="3468fbf0-8baa-4ea2-859b-bd0c70885ac2"><em>Scope</em> area (4) allows you to select the search scope of the elements related to the selected element.</li><li data-uuid="f9241358-689f-4037-bc4b-bf99e729569e"><em>Depth</em> area (5) allows you to specify the search range for the selected relationships.</li><li data-uuid="ccbb0544-ea3c-451f-8017-4a9bba69ed85">Always create new symbols option (6) allows you to create a symbol even if a related element already has a symbol in the diagram.</li><li data-uuid="f8beaef8-40cb-45b8-8393-f2549d952a79"><em>Layout related elements</em> option (7) lays out new symbols by using the default diagram layout.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog." ac:alt="The seven areas in the Display Related Elements dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_dialog_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The following procedure will guide you through using the <strong>Display Related Elements</strong> command and dialog when you want to represent all related elements via selected relationship types of the selected shape on the diagram pane. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736933 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03002: (2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically

- page_id: `272736933`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736933/2026x+Displaying+row+elements+hierarchically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:04.560+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables > (2026x) Basic tasks in tables > (2026x) Hierarchy in tables
- labels: ['nested-instances', 'display-nested-elements', 'display-nested-instances', 'display-mode', 'complete-tree', 'comapct-tree', 'specify-query', 'hierarchy-in-table', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Hierarchical tables are very useful for managing and reviewing data. Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.

**Important**

The hierarchy in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Instance table' space=''] is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.

##### Displaying elements hierarchically in a tableDisplaying elements hierarchically

To display elements hierarchically

1. In the table toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
2. Under the Display Mode option choose: The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree.
  - **Complete tree** to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - **Compact tree**to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying elements in custom hierarchyDisplaying row elements in a custom hierarchy

To display elements in a custom hierarchy

1. In the Criteria area, next to the Scope box, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='scope_query.png'] icon . [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_query_button.png']
2. Specify the expression to collect table elements:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

If you have selected Custom, please [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying expression in the Query dialog

##### Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table

To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table

- Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] / [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the name column. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="32a368a3-d9a8-4850-aab3-22a4c1917f37">Hierarchical</ac:inline-comment-marker> tables <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e7b8fd12-c662-412a-a1c0-e52637a8b825">are very useful for managing and reviewing data.</ac:inline-comment-marker> Hierarchy in a table is a tree-like structure where all the rows are listed according to the elements containment.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="92442073-15e8-48f5-ac69-a051ee9f98c7"><ac:rich-text-body><p class="title"><strong>Important</strong></p><p>The hierarchy in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Instance table" /></ac:link> is displayed according to an instance value that is a slot value of another Instance Specification. Composite instances are displayed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Displaying elements hierarchically in a table<ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa257eb0-0bb6-4a91-b1cf-ba7661e732b1"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying elements hierarchically</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></h3><p>To display elements hierarchically</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li><li>Under the <strong>Display Mode </strong>option choose:<br /><ul><li><p><strong>Complete tree</strong> to display row elements in a hierarchy where all the owners are represented in separate rows.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="complete_tree_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Compact tree </strong>to display row elements in a hierarchy where the common owners are grouped together in a separate row.  <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="compact_tree_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ul>The hierarchy is created in the table by listing all the elements as they are shown in the Containment tree. </li></ol><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="971ca2fc-d4ed-4689-89f2-4a7063d9aa81"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10fb336a-f2ff-45e9-9d06-9b128096c6a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying elements in custom hierarchy</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying row elements in a custom hierarchy</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d18be563-759f-4ea8-9c25-1e8527221d21">To display elements in a custom hierarchy</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>In the<strong> Criteria</strong> area, next to the <strong>Scope</strong> box, click the<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="scope_query.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>icon<strong>.</strong><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_query_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Specify the expression to collect table elements:</li></ol><ul><li>Select <strong>Containment</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Structure </strong>to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Custom</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice. </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9cbf8ae-93dc-456a-a5c9-b223978c97f5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have selected Custom, please <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify the expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_expression.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Specifying expression in the Query dialog</h6><h3>Expanding/collapsing nodes in a hierarchical table</h3><p>To expand/collapse nodes in a hierarchical table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>/<span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collapse.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span> in the name column.<br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"><ac:image ac:height="86"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expanding_nodes_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying row elements hierarchically" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></li><li>Select the specific row, hold Ctrl, and then press the right/left arrow key on the keyboard.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272736955 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03003: (2026x) Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row

- page_id: `272736955`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272736955/2026x+Displaying+hiding+element+numbers+in+a+row
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:48:07.771+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagrams > (2026x) Tables > (2026x) Basic tasks in tables > (2026x) Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.

To display/hide element numbers in the table

1. Open the Specification window of the table.
2. Set the **Show Element Number** property value to *true* to display element numbers, or *false* to hide them*.*The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example). [IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new table, elements represented as rows are automatically numbered in the name column. The following procedure is important for those who modify existing tables created before Version 18.4.</p><p>To display/hide element numbers in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Specification</strong> window of the table.</li><li><p>Set the <strong>Show Element Number</strong> property value to <em>true</em> to display element numbers, or <em>false</em> to hide them<em>.<br /></em>The element numbers are displayed in the name columns before the element name (see the following example).</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table." ac:alt="The Requirement numbers are displayed in the Magic Library Requirement table."><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_number_in_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Displaying/hiding element numbers in a row" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730797 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03004: (2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `272730797`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730797/2026x+DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:35.488+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393867802

393867804

393867803

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space=''] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space=''] inputs, outputs, and controls.

393867801

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating DIV-1 diagram' space='']

It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Information requirements.</li><li>Information hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity</span> definitions correlate to<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> information elements and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inputs, outputs, and controls.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f868ca3-1090-4090-850b-8b30b31e2f55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867801</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating DIV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735705 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03005: (2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `272735705`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735705/2026x+DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:40.687+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394056138

394056140

394056139

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='div_2.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space=''] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space=''].

The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space=''].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''].

394056137

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating DIV-2 diagram' space='']

Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.</p><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-2 Logical Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p>Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.</p><p>The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6c Event-Trace Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) StdV-1 Standards Profile" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65d590b1-f3f4-41f7-a0a6-4e3241ae8e59"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating DIV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735738 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03006: (2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `272735738`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735738/2026x+DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:45:46.466+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x) Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394180921

394180924

394180923

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''] is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition Diagram.

TheData Modelis one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) is actually implemented.

394180919

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Creating DIV-3 diagram' space='']

The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ac:link> is actually implemented.</p><p>While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.</p><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-3 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="DIV-3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-3 Physical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition Diagram.</li></ul><p>The<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Data Model </span>is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[DIV-2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) is actually implemented.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc0dfe18-156c-47fd-bc5a-aaaeaeeefe5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394180919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Creating DIV-3 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272730873 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03007: (2026x) Document Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `272730873`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272730873/2026x+Document+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:37:45.148+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983358622

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983358634

985841017

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983358624

#### NOTE: Note

Note

In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.

#### Using the Document Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select Report Designer Diagrams > Document Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating Report Designer Diagram.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as desired. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Document Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template** to generate the template. NoteOnly symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Report Generated from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Text Box

Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Text Box for holding text to be typed in.

###### Report Data Variable

Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the **Report Data Variable** dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.

###### Element Box

Element Box is represented as **#foreach** directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.

###### Page Box

Page Box is represented as **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the **Select Element** dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is **#foreach($class in $Class)**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.

###### Property Box

Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., $class.name or $class.isActive .
- If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., #foreach($member in $class.member) .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Box for obtaining an element property.

###### Element Table

Element Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Table** wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Table wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Table** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

After creating the Element Table, you can open the **Element Table** wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Table** editing context menu.

###### Property Table

Property Table is represented as **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the **Property Table** wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is **#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow**. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in ).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.

###### Include

Include is represented as **#include** and **#includeSection** directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Include/IncludeSection** dialog. After selecting the document, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****)** is created. There are two options to select an included document: **File** and **Attached File**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If you enter a section name in the **Section** box, **$import.include(****<document_name>.docx****,****<section_name>****)** will be created instead.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Include for including content or a document to the template.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.

###### Condition Box

Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Condition** dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press **CTRL** + **SPACE**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.
- If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.
- If you want to add an #else statement, select the Add #else option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Page Break for starting a new page.

#### Helper Tool

Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the **Helper Tool** dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the **Create this function in #foreach directive** option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.

983358620

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Report Designer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram' space='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bfd9d3e-138a-465d-83e0-3840e02074e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358622</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aef6c67-73f7-48d1-84e4-4247926d69c3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358634</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="beb0493c-6012-4059-b689-1de7e89f3156"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">985841017</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1ea7dc1-05bb-4e5f-8d69-19e5ea00f6be"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37f87569-bcb3-4f81-87a8-c6e645d24320"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358624</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="def2aaf1-2ee8-4c29-87ef-77edbcf43e0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In this version, Document Report Designer diagram supports only the diagram for making the Microsoft Word document (DOCX) template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using the Document Report Designer diagram</h2><p>In this section, you will learn how to create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a DOCX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the Document Report Designer diagram and generate it to a template</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a Package in the Containment tree, select <strong>Report Designer Diagrams</strong> &gt; <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating Report Designer Diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.</li><li>Arrange diagram elements as desired.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Arranging Diagram Elements.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Containment tree, right-click <strong>Document Report Designer Diagram</strong>  and select <strong>Generate Template</strong> to generate the template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bccdd6ce-5638-40b6-bb8e-811e1d2f858b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only symbols of elements on the diagram are used to generate the code template. Other elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate the code template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generating the Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li><li><p>Refine more layout, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.</p></li><li><p>Add the complete template to Report Wizard.</p></li><li><p>Generate the report from the added template.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Generated from Added Template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h2>Diagram elements</h2><p>Document Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.</p><h4>Text Box</h4><p>Text Box represents free, uninterpreted static-text area in which you can type anything in single or multiple lines, from plain-format text to Velocity code, one element per paragraph. You can also place Text Box anywhere.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Text Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Text Box for holding text to be typed in.</h6><h4>Report Data Variable</h4><p>Report Data Variable represents a variable of an existing Report Data model in a project. When you create the element in the diagram, the <strong>Report Data Variable</strong> dialog opens to select Report Data and select the variable of that Report Data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb7dd89e-a3ed-44f1-885d-9cdaa6a3bcd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Only Report Data in the Containment tree are supported.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Data Variable.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Report Data Variable and its dialog for selecting Report Data and their variables.</h6><h4>Element Box</h4><p>Element Box is represented as <strong>#foreach</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements. After creating the Element Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting a Class element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Box for fetching a collection of elements.</h6><h4>Page Box</h4><p>Page Box is represented as <strong>#forpage</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in each document page. After creating the Page Box in the diagram, you need to select an element type in the <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog. The statement for the PageBox element is <strong>#foreach($class in $Class)</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Box for fetching a collection of elements in each document page.</h6><h4>Property Box</h4><p>Property Box provides a way to get an element property. You can place this element in Element Box, Page Box, and Condition Box that is contained in Element Box. The property of owner's scope element will be listed in Property Box, by which the user selects the property to show in the group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4ad61ec-1f55-4a28-ae8d-e3eda91f5c62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the property value is a single item, the value will be printed in a single row, e.g., <strong>$class.name</strong> or <strong>$class.isActive</strong>.</li><li>If the property value is a collection, the value will be printed in multiple rows, e.g., <strong>#foreach($member in $class.member)</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Box for obtaining an element property.</h6><h4>Element Table</h4><p>Element Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Element Table for fetching a collection of elements in tabular form.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Element Table Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Element Table wizard dialog.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d00fcc8e-2369-4be6-b56b-88005db27ff8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In Step 2 of the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong> again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Guide Pop-up Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Property Guide pop-up dialog.</h6><p>You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Context Menu for Inserting and Deleting Rows and Columns.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.</h6><p>After creating the Element Table, you can open the <strong>Element Table</strong> wizard dialog again for updating the Element table through the smart manipulator button or the <strong>Element Table</strong> editing context menu.</p><h4>Property Table</h4><p>Property Table is represented as <strong>#forrow</strong> directive for fetching a collection of property elements in tabular form. You can place this Property Table in Element Box to open the <strong>Property Table</strong> wizard dialog to select the property scope of the Table element in the Specification dialog, e.g., the statement for selecting the member property of the Class element in the first row is <strong>#forrow($member in $class.member)$member.name#endrow</strong>. You can also add more lines and rows and create the header and border for the table (see the detail in <ac:link ac:anchor="ElementTable"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Element Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Table Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Property Table and the Property Table wizard dialog for selecting the property scope of the Table element.</h6><h4>Include</h4><p>Include is represented as <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#includeSection</strong> directives for including content or a document to the template. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Include/IncludeSection</strong> dialog. After selecting the document, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>)</strong> is created. There are two options to select an included document: <strong>File</strong> and <strong>Attached File</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db36aa00-e1fd-4c3b-b6fb-ff8cbadd6fb6"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you enter a section name in the <strong>Section</strong> box, <strong>$import.include(</strong><strong>&lt;document_name&gt;.docx</strong><strong>, </strong><strong>&lt;section_name&gt;</strong><strong>)</strong> will be created instead.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Include for including content or a document to the template.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Include Section Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Include/IncludeSection dialog for file or attached file selections.</h6><h4>Condition Box</h4><p>Condition Box is a container element for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Condition</strong> dialog. You can enter conditional statements into the statement fields. To guide available properties, you can type a dot (.) and press <strong>CTRL</strong> + <strong>SPACE</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2b279208-3639-46c7-b587-27a4774df765"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The #if statement is required for each Condition Box.</li><li>If you want to add more #elseif statements, you can add them by entering more conditional statements.</li><li>If you want to add an #else statement, select the <strong>Add #else</strong> option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Condition Box for storing #if, #elseif, and #else directives.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Condition Dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Condition dialog for entering conditional statements into the statement fields.</h6><h4>Page Break</h4><p>You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new page.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Page Break.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Page Break for starting a new page.</h6><h2>Helper Tool</h2><p>Helper Tool are a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates. Creating the element in the diagram opens the <strong>Helper Tool</strong> dialog. You must select or search for a purpose of the function in the list to display the function description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8fb04c8-a037-4c3e-9f6a-415ac170ff82"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the returned object is a collection of functions, select the <strong>Create this function in #foreach directive</strong> option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Helper Tool.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Document Report Designer diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Helper Tool as a collection of utilized functions for helping create templates.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="93dbb6b7-1bbc-4b38-a4d0-ca645b2887bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">983358620</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Report Designer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729863 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03008: (2026x) Documentation tab

- page_id: `272729863`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729863/2026x+Documentation+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:07.206+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Quick properties panel
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425887055

1425887058

1425887056

INLINE

The **Documentation** tab shows information associated with a selected model element in the Model Browser or on a diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Documentation** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Documentation tab.
- On the **Window** menu, click **Documentation**. If there is no documentation for the selected element, the text area is empty.

To write documentation for the selected model element

- In the Documentation tab, click the text area and type the text.

To turn on the HTML text

- In the Documentation tab, click the HTML check box.

The HTML toolbar opens and you can type the HTML text.

1425887054

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738934 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03009: (2026x) DOCX template in the Chart tool

- page_id: `272738934`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738934/2026x+DOCX+template+in+the+Chart+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:49.178+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Report Wizard > (2026x) Chart tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - "Column"
  - "Line"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"
- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (by default)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Line
    - "Line" (by default)
    - "3-D Line"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (by default)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (by default)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (by default)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (by default)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (by default)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

A code example:

```text

```

To set the chart title, use the following code:

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ff663a8-5a8f-4aaa-95a4-909cf4aa491e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span>If you do
