ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49b71698-6a7c-43f4-9c13-da1795a0b1d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart titles</h3><p>To set the chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e3e05969-ca81-46b0-b3e9-82e38d1e887a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed7a520-5006-41c6-a511-1d11e7c6b007"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting chart data categories</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data categories or row headers for the tag value of each data series/column by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7b64be4b-6660-487c-9bfe-246019575d19"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setCategories(categories : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b744b19c-7bee-444d-b0b3-f44cdc338507"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>From the sample code above, the row headers of the chart are set.</p><h3>Setting chart data series</h3><p>To create a matrix data table, set the data series or column headers by using the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9b6f0dd1-8866-40ee-93f4-9c71ce1b8768"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.createSeries(seriesName : String) : DataSeries]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28dc65b3-cf97-4b7f-938b-3103889f9435"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Setting a data source for each data series</h3><p>To prepare a data source for each of the data series, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7793b04-cb7a-4f69-916c-5e20fc74196e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setDataSources(dataSource : Collection)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f32c06d6-7451-4bc7-821d-d8583c928d39"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Printing a chart to a report</h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b60c62be-6788-4dcc-b7c4-1e45b0c57845"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b7d5a03-9b62-48e1-a357-7a3cfc23609b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sample Model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A sample model used to extract values for creating a chart.</h6><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values from a Model Mapped.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Tag values from a model mapped into a matrix table using the Chart tool to create a chart.</h6><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f8c30bd-fb4f-4f64-9858-69d2081bfa1e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Line", "Line"))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")

#set($categories = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $categories.add("TagA"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagB"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagC"))
#set($void = $categories.add("TagD"))

$linechart.setCategories($categories)
#foreach($element in $sorter.sort($MyElement))
	#set($series = $linechart.createSeries($element.name))
	#set($dataSources = $array.createArray())
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagA))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagB))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagC))
	#set($void = $dataSources.add("$element.tagD))
	#set($void = $series.setDataSources($dataSources))
#end
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Chart Printed.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) DOCX template in the Chart tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 03010: (2026x) DoDAF 2.0

- page_id: `272729035`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729035/2026x+DoDAF+2.0
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:25.883+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['dodaf', 'department-of-defense-architecture-framework', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229190542

229190544

229190543

The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding, and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.

This Architecture Framework is especially suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is apparently unique in its employment of "operational views". These views offer overview and details aimed to specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.

The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.

The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:

1. Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)
2. Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)
3. Defense Acquisition System (DAS)
4. Systems Engineering (SE)
5. Operational Planning (OPLAN)
6. Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)

According to modeling needs, there are the following DoDAF 2.0 templates for different purposes:

- **DoDAF 2.0 Project**- creates a blank DoDAF 2.0 project. creating a DoDAF 2.0 project will switch the application to the DoDAF 2.0 graphical user interface and use the DoDAF 2.0 model templates.
- **DoDAF Project**- creates a blank DoDAF project. creating a DoDAF project will switch the application to the DoDAF graphical user interface and use the DoDAF model templates.
- **DoDAF 2.0 Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new DoDAF 2.0 project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the DoDAF 2.0 structure and the architecture creation process.

The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229190541

**DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints**

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229241152

229241155

229241154

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

229241151

**Views**

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a18e5031-5281-4489-aef0-0e72b50557b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241152</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67d47093-d9e4-4231-9ff1-9adbc5b532a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241155</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05595130-662f-491f-98ab-01206508da0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241154</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8b8aee1-d337-4eef-9d7a-f2b7a26cfb63"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e656b9fb-5b2c-451d-baa9-7fa77cc43d2f" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272729085 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03012: (2026x) DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x

- page_id: `272729085`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272729085/2026x+DoDAF+Migration+to+UPDM+1.x
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:44.529+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.

The following chapters describe how the migration works.

##### Basic Concepts and Instructions

The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.

The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.

Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.</p><p>The following chapters describe how the migration works.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f5ba939c-91b7-4f76-908c-642fabb92d72" /></p><h3>Basic Concepts and Instructions</h3><p>The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.</p><p>The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.</p><p>Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738622 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03013: (2026x) DoDAF Projects Migration

- page_id: `272738622`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738622/2026x+DoDAF+Projects+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:25.627+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) Migration User Guides > (2026x) UPDM projects migration manual > (2026x) DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.

DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.

Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.

This section includes the following:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.</p><p>DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.</p><p>Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.</p><p>This section includes the following:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bf2daf1c-ca91-4be9-9f6b-c5c80be5bfc7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732822 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03014: (2026x) Dragging objects

- page_id: `272732822`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732822/2026x+Dragging+objects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:17.006+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['dragging-in-diagram', 'dragging-in-activity-diagram', 'dragging-in-state-machine-diagram', 'dragging-in-sequence-diagram', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

567604035

567604039

567604033

**On this page**

3

567604037

##### Dragging files and elements

The drag-and-drop capability allows you to:

- Drag files from your file system onto an element in the Model Browser or a symbol on a diagram. A hyperlink to the dragged file is automatically created.Dragged images are set as the **Image** property value of the element.If an image is dragged to a Stereotype, it is set as a Stereotype icon.
- Drag a project to the diagram pane to open the project.
- Drag source code files from your file system to the Code Engineering Set.
- Drag any elements to any properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='']. For example, you can drag a Class element from the Model Browser to the **Type** property in the **Operation** Specification window. That Class element is assigned as the Operation type in the Specification window.
- Drag any elements from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''] onto:
  - An empty place on a diagram. In this case, the class symbol is created on the diagram.
  - A shape on a diagram. In this case, a new attribute is created.
  - An element in the Model Browser. In this case, a new model element is created.
- Drag a property to create an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MED'] :
  - Drag a property from the classifier (for example, class, component, subsystem) compartment to a diagram pane.
  - Drag a property from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] to a diagram pane. In both cases, an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MED']is drawn from the classifier[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']to a property's type. The property itself is set as an association end. If the classifier symbol is not present on the diagram, it is added as a result of the created association.

If selected model elements are not compatible with an open diagram, you will not be allowed to drag those model elements.

##### Dragging in diagrams

You can drag-and-drop relationships to a diagram.The path for the relationship with shapes at the end of the path and the path between the shapes (if the shapes at the end of the path are already represented on the diagram) is created.

You can also drag-and-drop model elements to a note or a text box symbol in a diagram to add a hyperlink to that element.

###### Dragging in Sequence diagrams

In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Sequence diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'] from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED'] in a Sequence diagram.The Message becomes a Call Messageonce the operation is assigned. The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED']type must have or inherit this Operation.
- Drag an Interaction to an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction Use' space='MED']. The Interaction will be set as the Refers To element.
- Drag a Signal to a Message. The Message is converted into a Send Signal Message and the Signal is assigned to the Message.

###### Dragging in State Machine diagrams

- In a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) State Machine diagram' space=''] you can d rag an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MED'] element to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] element. A Trigger with this Event is created for the Transition element.
- You can drag behaviors from one state shape to another.

###### Dragging in Activity diagrams

In an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Activity diagram' space=''] you can:

- Drag an Activity, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MED'], or a State Machine onto an Activity diagram to create a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Call Behavior Action' space='MED']Call Behavior Action.
- Drag a Signal onto an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Send Signal Action' space='MED'].
- Drag a Signal onto a Send Signal Action to set or change the Signal.
- Drag a Signal onto an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MED'] to set or change the Signal. Once the Signal is assigned to the Accept Event Action, the Signal Event for the diagram and the Trigger for the action will be created automatically.
- Drag an Event onto an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag an Event onto an Accept Event Action to set the Event.
- Drag a Signal Event to an Activity diagram to create an Accept Event Action.
- Drag a Property, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] to an Activity diagram to create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']. Elements will be set as representative elements.
- Drag a Property, Actor, Class or Instance Specification to an Activity diagram by using the right mouse button. From the shortcut menu, select to create a Swimlane, Central Buffer Node, or available Actions.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732945 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03015: (2026x) Drawing diagram shapes

- page_id: `272732945`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732945/2026x+Drawing+diagram+shapes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:30.699+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568251568

568251570

568251569

You can draw diagram shapes in any type of diagram.

To display a diagram shape on a diagram pane

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Model Browser' space=''] , select the diagram.
2. Drag the diagram to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dgr_shape_on_dgr_pane.png']

You can change the representation of the diagram shape by changing the diagram stereotype display mode.

To change the representation of the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select a new value for the Show Stereotypes property. [ATTACHMENT filename='changing_dgr_shape_repres_sbml_prop.png']

You can also select whether to show or hide the diagram constraints or tagged values on the diagram shape.

To show diagram stereotype constraints and tagged values on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , select the Show Constraints and Show Tagged Values check boxes.

To display the context stereotype icon instead of the diagram stereotype icon on the diagram shape

1. From the diagram shape's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog , change the Use Stereotype property value to Context .

The **Use Stereotype** property takes effect only in case the **Show Stereotypes** property value is **Text and Icon** or **Icon**.

There is a possibility to show the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagram frame' space='']of a diagram type instead of the full diagram type on the diagram shape.

To display the abbreviated diagram type

- From the diagram shape shortcut menu, open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Symbol Properties dialog' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog and select the Show Abbreviated Type check box.

568251567

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Diagramming' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272739935 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03016: (2026x) EA with BPMN Project

- page_id: `272739935`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272739935/2026x+EA+with+BPMN+Project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:52:15.223+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

EA with BPMN Project is a template you can use if you want to work with a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling.

Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.

You can fing the sample project *Insurance Enterprise Architecture (EA-BPMN).mdzip*at <modeling tool installation folder>\samples\UAF

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">EA with BPMN Project is a template you can use if you want to work with a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26b1041f-5060-4d0a-ade0-ab73057f25a9"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can fing the sample project <em>Insurance Enterprise Architecture (EA-BPMN).mdzip </em>at &lt;modeling tool installation folder&gt;\samples\UAF</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="EA_simplified_framework.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) EA with BPMN Project" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732625 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03017: (2026x) Easier refactoring of composite used projects

- page_id: `272732625`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732625/2026x+Easier+refactoring+of+composite+used+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:40:59.509+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Project partitioning > (2026x) Automated project usages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Complex Composition

761371663

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Complex Composition

761371665

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Complex Composition

761371664

One of the problems caused by the old approach described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Automated project usages' space=''],was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected.

The following examples illustrate this problem.

#### INFO: Another types of problems

Example 1

Consider a used project *ElectricComponents* with 3 sub-used projects: *Capacitors*, *Inductances*, and *Resistances*. Consider another project *RadioSet*, which uses the used project *ElectricComponents*.

Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project *RadioSet*.

###### Old problematic behavior

Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:

*RadioSet*→*Capacitors,*

*RadioSet*→*Inductances,*and

*RadioSet*→*Resistances.*

Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project *ElectricComponents.*

If you tried to refactor the used project *ElectricComponents*by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project *RadioSet* would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects.

No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data.

###### New behavior

As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project *ElectricComponents* is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project *RadioSet* are automatically rearranged.

#### NOTE: Another types of problems

Another types of problems

#### INFO: Complex Composition

Example 2

Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project *RadioSet* uses the 100th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which in turn uses the 150th version of the sub-used project *Resistances.*

###### Old problematic behavior

Whenever the project *RadioSet* referenced the sub-used project *Resistances,* the direct usage would be created to the 150th version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project *RadioSet* to use the 200th version of the used project *ElectricComponents,* which used the 300th version of the sub-used project *Resistances,* a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150th version.

**New behavior**

As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.

#### NOTE: Complex Composition

Complex Composition

If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature.

761396174

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db9beb52-9ede-4aa7-86b4-e01ea55c580e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371663</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de9ee522-0a26-4b77-840d-bd5c83cee616"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371665</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="211f478b-40a3-40e6-9d47-89470b830f71"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761371664</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>One of the problems caused by the old approach described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Automated project usages" /></ac:link>, </span><span>was in refactoring composite used projects (used projects composed of sub-used projects). Whenever a composite used project was re-arranged by removing or re-importing sub-used projects, all of the projects or used projects using this used project more often than not became affected. </span></p><p><span>The following examples illustrate this problem. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ee5272f-9035-4b7b-af56-560a6b8307cb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Consider a used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> with 3 sub-used projects: <em>Capacitors</em>, <em>Inductances</em>, and <em>Resistances</em>. Consider another project <em>RadioSet</em>, which uses the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em>.</p><p>Now, use several model elements from each sub-used project in the project <em>RadioSet</em>.</p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p><span>Previous versions of our modeling tool would create these three direct user-defined usages:</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Capacitors, </em></span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Inductances, </em>and</span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><em>RadioSet</em> </span><span>→ </span><span><em>Resistances.</em> </span></p><p><span>Effectively, those versions were “freezing” the contents of the used project <em>ElectricComponents.</em></span></p><p><span>If you tried to refactor the used project <em>ElectricComponents </em>by removing its sub-used projects and re-importing their contents, the project <em>RadioSet</em> would be affected and start complaining about the missing used projects. </span></p><p><span>No earlier version of our modeling tool could rearrange the user-defined usages for fear of losing the user-specified used project composition data. </span></p><h4><span>New behavior </span></h4><p><span>As of version 17.0.3, a program creates automated used project usages instead of creating user-defined ones. So when the used project <em>ElectricComponents</em> is refactored, it keeps the same model elements but changes its structure. Furthermore, the automated usages in the project <em>RadioSet</em> are automatically rearranged. </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2eb0a945-9776-4277-a14d-78b7adbb4fac"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Another types of problems</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Now problems could occur only if the rearranged used project does not contain all the same elements it had before the rearrangement. Thus some automated usages may turn into unconfirmed ones. For more information about unconfirmed usages, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Controlling dependency creation between used projects" /></ac:link>. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f7706641-5f41-4d39-ac76-ab0415ad651d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Another problem was “freezing” the used project's version. For instance, the project <em>RadioSet</em> uses the 100<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which in turn uses the 150<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances.</em></p><h4>Old problematic behavior</h4><p>Whenever the project <em>RadioSet</em> referenced the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> the direct usage would be created to the 150<sup>th</sup> version of it. If you wanted to migrate the project <em>RadioSet</em> to use the 200<sup>th</sup> version of the used project <em>ElectricComponents,</em> which used the 300<sup>th</sup> version of the sub-used project <em>Resistances,</em> a version conflict would occur, since the direct usage would be still pinned to the 150<sup>th</sup> version.</p><p><strong>New behavior</strong></p><p>As of version 17.0.3, automated usages do not carry information about the version and do not preclude the version update through the user-defined usage paths.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6abb8f3-6263-4d53-8b06-52a3b73f9e93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Complex Composition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>If you already have projects with complex composition (having used projects or sub-used projects) that are prepared in any earlier version, you may want to refactor your used project structure to reap the benefits of this feature. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f625112b-15a2-469a-8aaf-e30e9f7f088f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">761396174</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731994 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03018: (2026x) Ecore modeling

- page_id: `272731994`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731994/2026x+Ecore+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:59.596+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects > (2026x) Ecore support
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Diagrams and elements in Ecore models

There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.

Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.

Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.

Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.

There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their **opposite** property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::**eSuperTypes** property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.

Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, **SortedList<T extends Comparable<? super T>>**. 
Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.

##### Element properties in Ecore models

There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:

- The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix ( Namespace Prefix ) and nsURI (corresponds to the URI property specified in the UML v2.4.1). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_package.png']

- The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName ( Instance Class Name ) and instanceTypeName ( Instance Type Name ). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png']

- Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile ( Volatile ), transient ( Transient ), unsettable ( Unsettable ), and resolveProxies ( Resolve Proxies , used for references only). [ATTACHMENT filename='additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png']

##### Annotation modeling

Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.

However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. 
 
To create an Ecore annotation

1. In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.
2. Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotations_specification_window.png']

To create a key-value pair

1. In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the Annotation Details property group. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_details.png']
2. Click the Create button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_string_to_string.png']
3. Enter values for both the Value and Key properties.
4. Click the Back button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. [ATTACHMENT filename='ecore_annotation_vision.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Diagrams and elements in Ecore models</h3><p>There are no specific diagrams for editing Ecore models in the modeling tool. You can use the same Class diagrams as you use for your UML models. Since Ecore is almost a subset of UML (with a few additions), familiar UML elements are used for the modeling. You can also develop Ecore models without using the Ecore profile at all. If your Ecore model uses only UML-specific information, you can develop it using plain UML and export it to Ecore without any problem.</p><p>Ecore is even more similar to EMOF. You can export the same model to both Ecore and EMOF.</p><p>Class, DataType, Enumeration, Package, Operation, Parameter have a direct one-to-one correspondence between UML and Ecore.</p><p>Ecore has two flavors of structural features, EAttribute and EReference, while UML has just one – Property. Fortunately a differentiation between an attribute and a reference is unambiguous and automatically resolved: the property, whose type is a data type is treated as EAttribute; the property, whose type is a class is treated as EReference. Hence the user does not need to worry about this - he/she can simply use properties.</p><p>There are no standalone Association and Generalization model elements in Ecore, but there is analogous information in Ecore: two EReferences, pointing to each other by their <strong>opposite</strong> property is equivalent to the association; the EClass::<strong>eSuperTypes</strong> property is equivalent to a generalization. Hence it is possible and meaningful to draw associations and generalizations in your model for exporting this information to Ecore.</p><p>Ecore generics (templates) are also supported. You can use the UML template support to model Ecore generics. While the modeling is not trivial (and not one-to-one due to weak semantics of Ecore's EGenericType), it is possible to model all cases of template types, even ones with complexly nested type bounds like, for example, <strong>SortedList&lt;T extends Comparable&lt;? super T&gt;&gt;</strong>. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />Your models can also contain any other UML elements, which are not present in Ecore. These elements are simply skipped during the export to Ecore. A warning is given about these elements.</p><h3>Element properties in Ecore models</h3><p>There are few Ecore-specific properties, which are brought in when the Ecore profile is used. These properties are used to capture Ecore specific information, not existing in UML. MOF-specific properties are also relevant for Ecore. These special properties are as follows:</p><ul><li>The Ecore package has the additional properties: nsPrefix (<strong>Namespace Prefix</strong>) and nsURI (corresponds to the <strong>URI</strong> property specified in the UML v2.4.1).<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_package.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>The Ecore classifier (class, data type, enumeration) has the additional properties: instanceClassName (<strong>Instance Class Name</strong>) and instanceTypeName (<strong>Instance Type Name</strong>). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_clasiffier.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>Ecore attributes and references (modeled as the UML property) have the additional properties: volatile (<strong>Volatile</strong>), transient (<strong>Transient</strong>), unsettable (<strong>Unsettable</strong>), and resolveProxies (<strong>Resolve Proxies</strong>, used for references only). <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="additional_properties_ecore_attribute.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Annotation modeling</h3><p>Ecore annotations are modeled as UML comments. For simple annotations no additional actions are necessary.</p><p>However, Ecore annotations have more powerful semantic than UML comments – they can have an internal substructure. In particular they can have an additional key-value map. For this additional information, there is a special «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype, that can be applied on an annotating comment. After applying the stereotype, the key-value map can be entered in a separate node of the annotating comment Specification window. Key-value pairs are stored as internal subcomment elements of the annotation. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create an Ecore annotation</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore model, create a Comment element.</li><li>Apply the «EcoreAnnotation» stereotype to the element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotations_specification_window.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To create a key-value pair</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Ecore annotation's Specification window, select the <strong>Annotation Details</strong> property group. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_details.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click the <strong>Create</strong> button. The key-value pair's Specification window opens. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_string_to_string.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Enter values for both the <strong>Value</strong> and <strong>Key</strong> properties.</li><li>Click the <strong>Back</strong> button when you are done. You will see the key-value pair created. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ecore_annotation_vision.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Ecore modeling" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272731992 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03019: (2026x) Ecore support

- page_id: `272731992`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272731992/2026x+Ecore+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:39:59.026+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305838392

305838394

305838393

Ecore model is a model type supported by Eclipse Modeling Framework (EMF). This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).

Ecore models can be used for various purposes:

- Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).
- Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.

Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.

305838390

**Related pages**

****

This model type can be colloquially called the EMF model (even though EMF supports many types of models, e.g., UML models).</p><p>Ecore models can be used for various purposes:</p><ul><li>Metamodeling purposes, where its expressive power is roughly similar to EMOF (and even slightly higher than EMOF).</li><li>Simple class modeling purposes, where Ecore model is used as a subset of UML.</li></ul><p>Ecore models made using the modeling tool can be exported as Ecore models for the further processing (generating model repositories, code or XML parsing and storing, etc.) with other EMF tools.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13b48495-04a9-4916-b419-21693e6a1a0a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305838390</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="adfefb38-4bfc-49a1-b3bc-f6d93ba5028e" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735942 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 03020: (2026x) Edit Hyperlink dialog

- page_id: `272735942`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735942/2026x+Edit+Hyperlink+dialog
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:42:50.140+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Manage Navigation > (2026x) Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add new or manage existing hyperlinks in the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog, consisting of:

- Three main tabs containing all modeling tools: **Address** tab, **File** tab, and **Element/Symbol** tab.
- One tab required to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin : **OSLC Link** tab .

The following figure illustrates the tabs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All available tabs in the Edit Hyperlink dialog.

##### Hyperlink types

The following table provides details of available hyperlink types.

| Tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog | Hyperlink type | Required plugins | Tab descriptions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Address tab | Links to webpages or other external resources. | - | Learn more about Address tab >> |
| File tab | Opens the file from your file system. | Learn more about File tab >> |  |
| Element/Symbol tab | In HTML text, opens the element Specification window.In plain text, on element/symbol, or diagram, opens the diagram. | Learn more about Element/Symbol tab >> |  |
| OSLC Link tab | Links to OSLC resources. | Cameo DataHub Plugin. How to install the Cameo DataHub Plugin >> | Learn more about OSLC Link tab >> |

##### Opening the Edit Hyperlink dialog

To open the **Edit Hyperlink** dialog

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Hyperlinks in texts' space='']>]]>
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Hyperlinks on elements' space='']>]]>

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Linking model elements to OSLC resources
- Managing hyperlinks

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272728987 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03021: (2026x) Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities dialog

- page_id: `272728987`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272728987/2026x+Edit+Item+Flow+Realization+Between+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:33:11.816+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Item Flow management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog allows you to review Activities for a selected Item Flow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Item Flow, select From Activity or To Activity property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Item Flow Manager dialog' space='']**Item Flow Manager** dialog , select the From Activity or To Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete the selected Activity pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Activity pairs

1. Select an Activity pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272738500 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03022: (2026x) Edit Project Status in Lr diagram

- page_id: `272738500`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272738500/2026x+Edit+Project+Status+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:50:16.235+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x) NAF 4.0 > (2026x) Logical viewpoint > (2026x) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['apply-project-status-to-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit the Project Status

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> opens.</li><li><p class="confluence-link">Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734344 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03023: (2026x) Editing logical values

- page_id: `272734344`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734344/2026x+Editing+logical+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:48.556+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Editing property values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Logical (boolean) type property value can be either *true*or *false*. Examples of logical type properties: *Is Abstract*, *Is Read Only*.

To edit a logical property value, do one of the following

- Select the check box to set the value to *true*.
- Clear the check box selection to set value to *false*.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing property values' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734628 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03024: (2026x) Editing long textual values

- page_id: `272734628`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734628/2026x+Editing+long+textual+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:10.286+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Editing property values > (2026x) Editing textual properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In case of editing a value that consists of several words, the value cell may seem too short. In order to avoid this inconvenience you can edit the property value in a dialog specially designated for typing long values.

To edit a textual value consisting of several words

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the opened dialog, type a value and click **OK**.

There are some textual properties, whose values can keep text formatting settings. You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) HTML editor' space='']HTML editorin such cases. To use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) HTML editor' space=''], select the **HTML**check box in the dialog opened for editing this special kind of textual property value.

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing textual properties' space='']modified1

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:27.690+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Editing property values > (2026x) Editing textual properties
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some properties can have more than one textual value, for example, the **Body**property of an opaque expression, the **Pre Condition**and **Post Condition**properties of a use case. You can use a special editor to edit multiline textual values.

Using the editor for multiline textual values, you can perform the following actions:

- Create a new value.
- Remove a selected value.
- Edit each value in the HTML editor separately.
- Reorder values.
- Strip long values (available only in the Specification window).

To strip long values, make sure that the Strip Multiline Text mode isturned on in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space=''].

The following table describes functions of buttons used in the editor.

| Button | Shortcut Key | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Select the HTML check box to edit a selected row in the HTML mode.The HTML editor toolbar will appear. For more information about theHTML toolbar, refer to "HTML editor toolbar" on page 428. |
|  | ALT+ENTER | Click to add an empty row after the selected one to type a new value. |
|  | ALT+BACKSPACE | Click to remove a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+UPARROW | Click to moves up a selected row. |
|  | ALT+SHIFT+DOWNARROW | Click to moves down a selected row. |

Mac OS users should use MAC shortcut key as an alternative for the ALT key.

## PAGE 03026: (2026x) Editing property values

- page_id: `272734325`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734325/2026x+Editing+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:46.484+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can edit property values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Specification window' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Tables' space='']
- Element tab on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Properties tab' space='']**Properties**tabin the Model Browser

Property values in gray are non-editable. They are derived from other elements used in your model.

Learn about editing values of different property types in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing textual properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing logical values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Cases of advanced editing' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272735888 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03027: (2026x) Editing property values in property groups

- page_id: `272735888`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272735888/2026x+Editing+property+values+in+property+groups
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:46:21.397+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Editing property values > (2026x) Cases of advanced editing
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Some elements can be related to each other as the owner and the owned one, for example, a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] can be an owner of a trigger, and the trigger can be owned by the transition.

The modeling tool allows the handy editing of owned element property values via owner’s properties. Owned element properties are available as appropriately named property groups, for example, the *Entry*, *Do Activity*, and *Exit*property groups in the state’s Specification window (see the following figure).

When editing owned element properties, keep in mind that you create a new element in your model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A property setin the property group may vary before and after owned element specification. For example, let’s say,we have an activity assigned as a behavior type in the **Entry**property group. As a result the additional properties appear in the **Entry** property group (**Name** and **Owned Diagram**) after creating a new activity (see the following figure).

Keep in mind that the owner’s specification contains only ownership-relevant properties of an owned element. 
To view and edit all other properties of the owned element, open its Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an owned element as a property value

1. In the property group, wherein you want to create the owned element, select either a type or an elementproperty specification cell.
2. From the list, select an element you need to create.
3. Specify additional properties corresponding to the created element.

To remove an owned element, do one of the following

- Either

1. Select the **<owned element type>**property specification cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

- Or

1. Select the <owned element> property cell and click **...** button.
2. From the list, select **Delete <element>**.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734352 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03028: (2026x) Editing textual properties

- page_id: `272734352`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734352/2026x+Editing+textual+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:43:49.889+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Editing property values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Textual type property values are usually simply typed in a property value cell. In some specific cases you can also use advanced editing features, i.e., long textual values editor and multiline textual values editor (both available in HTML). 
Examples of textual type properties: *Name*, *Body*.

To edit a textual property value

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Type a value.

Learn about advanced cases of editing textual values in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing long textual values' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing multiline textual values' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Editing property values' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272737914 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03029: (2026x) Editor Tabs

- page_id: `272737914`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272737914/2026x+Editor+Tabs
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:49:22.943+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Editor tabs allow you to access diagrams and model samples.

On this page:

##### Editor tab types

The modeling tool contains the following editor tabs:

- Welcome tab. The welcome tab is displayed once the modeling tool is launched. It allows you to create new or open other projects, view model samples provided with the modeling tool installation, etc.
- Samples tab. Displays the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation.
- Diagram Editor tab. It allows you to display and work with diagrams. You can have multiple diagram tabs open simultaneously.

##### Editor tabs commands

Editor tabs' commands can be found:

- In the Main Menu , click Window , then Editor Tabs . In the dropdown menu, select the appropriate command.

##### Viewing samples

You can view the model samples provided with the modeling tool installation either through the Samples tab or the Welcome tab.

###### Viewing samples in the Welcome tab

To view samples in the Welcome tab

- Once the modeling tool is launched, in the Welcome tab, click Samples .

###### Invoking the samples tab

To invoke the samples tab

- In the Main Menu , click Help , then Show Samples .

##### Navigating content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

You can use the search bar to navigate the content in the Welcome and Samples tabs. This is especially useful for locating specific samples.

To navigate content in the Welcome or Samples tabs

1. Use the Ctrl+F shortcut keys to invoke the search bar.
2. In the Find input field, type the search keyword.
3. (Optional) Use the commands in the search bar for efficient search.

The table below displays the search bar's commands.

| Command icon | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Click to close the search bar. |
|  | Type the search keyword(s). |
|  | Click to highlight the next search result. |
|  | Click to highlight the previous search result. |
|  | Click to highlight all search results. |
|  | Select the check box to find results matching the search input letter case. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272733009 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 03031: (2026x) Electrical Circuit

- page_id: `272733009`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272733009/2026x+Electrical+Circuit
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-11-25T14:41:59.493+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x) Modelica export > (2026x) Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa', 'unrestored-unknown-attachment']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate *ElectricCircuit* example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Electric Circuit sample' space='']Learn more about *ElectricCircuit* sample model >>

You can find the *ElectricCircuit* sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > ElectricCircuit.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*ElectricCircuit.*

To simulate the Circuit Modelica model

1. Export the Circuit Block to Modelica file. How to >>
2. Make sure the Dymola tool is installed. How to >>
3. Start the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select saved *Circuit.mo* file from your file directory. A*Circuit*model is displayed in the **Package Browser**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the **Graphics**tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to check model for syntax and semantic errors. The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: 
 a. Select the******Simulation**tab and in its toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. b. In the**Simulation Setup**dialog, specifythe**Start time******value to**0**and the**Stop time******value to**60**seconds(or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 60 seconds is enough). c. Click**OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *Circuit*variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see input and output of the Circuit model, select variables in the Variable Browser as follows: Circuit > _Circuit > c, i, rc, ri, s > iThru , vDrop . T he current ( iThru ) and voltage ( vDrop ) variable across each of the circuit’s 5 components are displayed on the **Plot**window: the capacitor ( c ), the inductor ( i ), the voltage source ( v ), and the resistors ( rc, ri ). In the example below, only iThru , vDrop of s variable are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='plot_diagram_s_variable.png']
