<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272734798 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03117: (2026x) Filter By Package Imports filter

- page_id: `272734798`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272734798/2026x+Filter+By+Package+Imports+filter
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:44:28.520+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with model elements > (2026x) Selecting elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1038774317

1038774329

1038774315

**On this page**

****

1038774319

##### Restricting type selection list to imported elements

To restrict the type selection list to imported elements

1. Create a Package diagram.
2. Drag the package with typed elements, as well as packages with types, on the diagram pane.
3. Create a package import between them by clicking Package Import in the diagram palette. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_package_import_button.png']

To apply the filter

1. Click a typed element to open the **Select Type** dialog.
2. Apply the **Filter By Package Imports filter**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As a result, the type selection list is restricted to imported elements only.

There are more [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting elements' space=''] you can use for the same purpose.

##### Different abstraction levels

You can also use a Model instead of a package, in which case, the elements cannot be accessed from outside, and you can only select the types from packages that are owned within the Model element.

To only show elements from the desired abstraction level

1. Right-click the package to which you want to restrict your type selection, and select Refactor > Convert To > Model .
2. Select a typed element nested in that model, and specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_port_type.png']
3. Apply Filter By Package Imports to only list the elements that belong to the same abstraction level.

##### Validation

Use active validation to evaluate which element types are out of the scope specified by package imports.

1. Go to Analyze > Validation > Validate.
2. In the Validation dialog , select UML correctness constraints from the Validation Suite drop-down list.
3. Click Validate .
4. In the Validation Results panel, right-click the element you want to change or unset type for, and select Change Type , Unset Type , or Create Package Import Relationship accordingly. [ATTACHMENT filename='elements_out_of_scope.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8eed513e-36f9-44fc-a712-6be5295552e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1038774317</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65ceea44-eddc-4bfb-a805-b15a84e935f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1038774329</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="282c64e4-39ab-48d5-b9a5-2399d1faa81d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1038774315</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1efa16c-e58e-4cd7-9e2e-3e4dcbb531ab" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4ea5d74f-17a3-452a-ba3f-d3b663568c27"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1038774319</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Restricting type selection list to imported elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>To restrict the type selection list to imported elements</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Package diagram.</li><li><p>Drag the package with typed elements, as well as packages with types, on the diagram pane.</p></li><li>Create a package import between them by clicking <strong>Package Import</strong> in the diagram palette. <br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_package_import_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filter By Package Imports filter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To apply the filter</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click a typed element to open the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog.</p></li><li><p>Apply the <strong>Filter By Package Imports filter</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="apply_filter_by_package_imports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filter By Package Imports filter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p>As a result, the type selection list is restricted to imported elements only.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f93b80fc-19f2-4f52-a91d-b488b373f762"><ac:rich-text-body><p>There are more <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[useful filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you can use for the same purpose.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Different abstraction levels</h3><p>You can also use a Model instead of a package, in which case, the elements cannot be accessed from outside, and you can only select the types from packages that are owned within the Model element.</p><p><br /></p><p>To only show elements from the desired abstraction level</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the package to which you want to restrict your type selection, and select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; <strong>Model</strong>.</li><li>Select a typed element nested in that model, and specify its type.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_port_type.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filter By Package Imports filter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Apply <strong>Filter By Package Imports</strong> to only list the elements that belong to the same abstraction level.<br /><br /></li></ol><h3>Validation</h3><p>Use active validation to evaluate which element types are out of the scope specified by package imports.</p><ol><li>Go to <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>Validation </strong>&gt;<strong> Validate.</strong></li><li>In the<strong> Validation </strong>dialog<strong>, </strong>select<strong> </strong><strong>UML correctness constraints</strong> from the <strong>Validation Suite</strong> drop-down list.</li><li>Click <strong>Validate</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, right-click the element you want to change or unset type for, and select <strong>Change Type</strong>, <strong>Unset Type</strong>, or <strong>Create Package Import Relationship</strong> accordingly. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elements_out_of_scope.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filter By Package Imports filter" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=272732671 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 03118: (2026x) Filtering

- page_id: `272732671`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/272732671/2026x+Filtering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-11-25T13:41:09.789+01:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x) General modeling features > (2026x) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

632682683

632682687

632682686

INLINE

The modeling tool filtering mechanism allows filtering types of model elements in particular lists. For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.

To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Containment tab' space='']**Containment** tab

1. In the Model Browser, open the Containment tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button_in_model_browser.png'] and select Filter . [ATTACHMENT filename='containment_tab_filter.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting element types' space=''] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_type_dialog.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the Containment tab.
  - Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the **Containment** tab. An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the **Hide Metatype** property set to *true*in its customization.
4. Click OK . Items in the Containment tab are now filtered.

Containment

Model

Containment

To filter the types of model elements when [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Searching' space='']

1. From the Edit main menu, select Find . The Find dialog opens.
2. Next to the Type box, click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting element types' space=''] dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png']
3. Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.
4. Click OK . You are now back to the Find dialog then click Find . The Search Results tab opens in the Model Browser.

632682682

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x) Selecting element types' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ba8e0e1-ad64-4487-9aea-bf8c80ec0c34"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682683</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2f3315c4-325c-4902-af5e-d1a718016309"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682687</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d0d20d3-0f30-4018-a5cf-8de7bf7d6e5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682686</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b20ccd72-14ba-45f7-9e4e-4a90a22339ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The modeling tool filtering mechanism allows filtering types of model elements in particular lists. For example, you can filter the types of model elements that are displayed in the Model Browser, or you can select them when searching.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To filter the types of the model elements that are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Containment tab" /><ac:link-body><strong>Containment</strong> tab</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Containment</strong> tab (in case it was closed or another tab was open).</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button_in_model_browser.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Filter</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="containment_tab_filter.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br />The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Selecting element types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_type_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Click to clear the check box next to the model element type, which you do not want to be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click to select the check box next to the model element type, which you want to be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24dfbfa7-b88f-41ad-be30-c4af96af58c4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An element cannot be filtered if it has a stereotype applied to it, too. You can only filter elements that have a custom stereotype applied, under the condition that the stereotype has the <strong>Hide Metatype</strong> property set to <em>true </em>in its customization.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span>Click <strong>OK</strong>. Items in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are now filtered.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63141028-7df0-4345-b256-5b4121ee2267"><ac:rich-text-body>All elements in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab are displayed in hierarchical order. If you clear the <em>Model</em> check box, no elements will be displayed in the <strong>Containment</strong> tab. The same is valid for all element types – if you clear the check box next to the type of element that has inner elements in the Model Browser, you won't be able to see these inner elements.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To filter the types of model elements when <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Searching" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[searching]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, select <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Find</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Next to the <strong>Type</strong> box, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Selecting element types" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element Type]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_element_tyle_dialog_actor.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Filtering" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click to select the check box of the model element type that you want to search for.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. You are now back to the <strong>Find</strong> dialog then click <strong>Find</strong>. The <strong>Search </strong><strong>Results</strong> tab opens in the Model Browser.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="087068b7-4811-4101-9f5a-6bf4b2630565"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">632682682</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Selecting element types" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````
